items.title: Provisioning flow does not generate or store SSH keypair — FEAT-005 deploy update is undeliverable
items.intent: Every provisioned cloud agent has an SSH keypair generated at provision time, stored securely, and linked in the deploy state. clawhq deploy update has a viable SSH path to every cloud agent it created.
items.description: ProvisionedInstance interface (src/cloud/provisioning/types.ts) has no sshKeyPath or keypairPath field. The provisioning wizard does not generate an SSH keypair or inject it via cloud-init. Without a keypair, clawhq deploy update --id <agent-id> has no way to connect to the remote agent — FEAT-005 is architecturally undeliverable until this is resolved. Steps: (1) Add sshKeyPath: string field to ProvisionedInstance interface. (2) During provisioning wizard, generate an Ed25519 keypair (ssh-keygen or node:crypto), store private key at ~/.clawhq/keys/<agent-id>.pem (mode 0600), store public key path in ProvisionedInstance.sshKeyPath. (3) Inject public key via cloud-init user_data (authorized_keys). (4) Save sshKeyPath to deploy state file on disk. (5) Run cloud/provisioning tests. Scope: types.ts + provisioning wizard + 1-2 provider files for cloud-init injection. No logic change to existing deploy flow.
items.groomingNotes: READY. ProvisionedInstance confirmed in types.ts:285-306 — no sshKeyPath field exists. wizard.ts confirmed no keypair generation code. cloud-init.ts has generateCloudInit function needing pubKey param. Scope: 3 files (types.ts, wizard.ts, cloud-init.ts). node:crypto is built-in — no new deps. Steps prescriptive with file locations. Unblocks FEAT-005.

items.title: AWS listSshKeys() returns empty publicKey — silent data loss in SshKeyInfo interface
items.intent: Every call to listSshKeys() returns structurally honest data — callers that check publicKey will not silently receive an empty string and mishandle it.
items.description: aws.ts line 301 returns publicKey: '' because AWS EC2 API does not expose public key material after initial import, only fingerprints. The SshKeyInfo interface (types.ts:214-220) defines publicKey: string with no optional marker, so callers expect a real value. Steps: (1) Read src/cloud/provisioning/providers/aws.ts lines 292-310 (listSshKeys implementation). (2) Read src/cloud/provisioning/types.ts lines 214-220 (SshKeyInfo interface). (3) Make publicKey optional in SshKeyInfo interface (publicKey?: string). (4) Update the AWS adapter to omit publicKey or set it to undefined rather than empty string. (5) Check all callers of listSshKeys() for publicKey usage — grep for .publicKey in provisioning/. (6) Update any callers that assume publicKey is always truthy to handle undefined. (7) Run cloud/provisioning tests.
steps: Read src/cloud/provisioning/providers/aws.ts lines 292-310 to confirm listSshKeys() returns publicKey: ''
Read src/cloud/provisioning/types.ts lines 214-220 to confirm SshKeyInfo.publicKey type and optionality
Grep for .publicKey in src/cloud/provisioning/ to find all callers that read the field
Update SshKeyInfo interface: change publicKey: string to publicKey?: string
Update aws.ts listSshKeys(): omit publicKey field or set to undefined rather than empty string
Update any caller that assumes publicKey is always truthy to handle undefined
Run existing cloud/provisioning tests — confirm no regressions
acceptanceCriteria: SshKeyInfo.publicKey is typed as optional (publicKey?: string) in types.ts
AWS listSshKeys() no longer returns publicKey: '' — field is omitted or undefined
DigitalOcean, GCP, Hetzner listSshKeys() implementations unchanged and return real key values
No callers of listSshKeys() assume publicKey is always a non-empty string without a guard
items.groomingNotes: READY. aws.ts:301 confirmed returning publicKey: '' by inspection. SshKeyInfo.publicKey: string confirmed at types.ts:214-220. DigitalOcean, GCP, Hetzner all return real key values — AWS is the outlier. Fix is local to aws.ts + types.ts. No new deps. Scope: 2 files.

items.title: Instance registry file written without mode 0600 — world-readable on disk
items.intent: The instance registry is written with mode 0600 — only the owning user can read it.
items.description: src/cloud/provisioning/registry.ts line 63 writes the instance registry file using default filesystem permissions. Unlike credentials.json which uses FILE_MODE_SECRET (0600), the registry uses no explicit mode. The registry contains provisioned instance data including IP addresses, provider IDs, and (after BUG-072) sshKeyPath. This data should be owner-read-only.
steps: Read src/cloud/provisioning/registry.ts — find the file write call (around line 63)
Grep for FILE_MODE_SECRET in the codebase to find where 0600 is defined and how it is applied to credentials.json
Apply FILE_MODE_SECRET (or equivalent 0600) to the registry file write — match pattern used for credentials.json
Confirm no other registry read/write paths set a permissive mode
acceptanceCriteria: Instance registry file is written with mode 0600
Mode is applied using the same constant as credentials.json — no magic number
All four provider adapters tested confirm registry is written with 0600 after provisioning
items.groomingNotes: RE-GROOMED 2026-03-23 (priority bump). World-readable file containing instance data (IPs, provider IDs, and after BUG-072: SSH key paths) violates the security-by-default non-negotiable in CLAUDE.md. credentials.json uses FILE_MODE_SECRET (0600) — the registry must match. Bumped from should → must: hardening is automatic, not opt-in. Fix is 1 file, 1 line. No excuse to defer.

items.title: Cloud adapter initialization exceptions not caught in resolveAdapter() — crashes on bad credentials
items.intent: A malformed stored credential produces a clear, actionable error message — never an uncaught exception stack trace.
items.description: src/cloud/provisioning/engine.ts lines 62-73 call createAwsAdapter() and createGcpAdapter() which can throw exceptions during token parsing (malformed JSON, missing fields). These calls are not wrapped in try-catch. If a user's stored token is malformed, the error propagates uncaught and crashes the provisioning flow without a helpful error message. DigitalOcean and Hetzner adapters do not have this issue — only AWS and GCP. Steps: (1) Read src/cloud/provisioning/engine.ts around lines 62-73 to see resolveAdapter() implementation. (2) Read src/cloud/provisioning/providers/aws.ts createAwsAdapter() and src/cloud/provisioning/providers/gcp.ts createGcpAdapter() to understand what exceptions they throw. (3) Wrap the adapter creation calls in try-catch in resolveAdapter(). (4) On catch, throw a descriptive error: 'Failed to initialize AWS/GCP adapter: credentials may be malformed. Re-run clawhq creds to reconfigure.'
steps: Read src/cloud/provisioning/engine.ts lines 62-73 — confirm createAwsAdapter() and createGcpAdapter() are called without try-catch
Read src/cloud/provisioning/providers/aws.ts createAwsAdapter() to identify what exceptions it throws and under what conditions
Read src/cloud/provisioning/providers/gcp.ts createGcpAdapter() to identify what exceptions it throws and under what conditions
Wrap createAwsAdapter() call in try-catch — on catch, throw new Error with message: 'Failed to initialize AWS adapter: credentials may be malformed. Re-run clawhq creds to reconfigure.'
Wrap createGcpAdapter() call in try-catch — on catch, throw new Error with message: 'Failed to initialize GCP adapter: service account JSON may be malformed. Re-run clawhq creds to reconfigure.'
Confirm DigitalOcean and Hetzner adapter initialization already handles errors gracefully
acceptanceCriteria: Calling resolveAdapter() with a malformed AWS token throws a descriptive Error, not an uncaught exception
Calling resolveAdapter() with malformed GCP service account JSON throws a descriptive Error, not an uncaught exception
Error messages reference 'clawhq creds' as the remediation path
DigitalOcean and Hetzner adapter init behavior is unchanged
items.groomingNotes: READY. engine.ts:62-73 confirmed calling createAwsAdapter() and createGcpAdapter() without try-catch via codebase inspection. Both adapters do token parsing that can throw. Fix is local to engine.ts — 2 try-catch blocks. No new deps. Scope: 1 file (engine.ts).
